title: -Contract: Paymun Building & Development, Inc. for the K-Dock Restroom Renovation Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving the plans and specifications for the K-Dock Restroom Renovation
Project (Bid Specification No. 25-11743-C); and
2. Rejecting the bid from ERA Construction, Inc., the fourth lowest bidder and deem
ERA Construction, Inc. ineligible to protest against Argos Construction, apparent
lowest bidder, Paymun Building & Development, Inc., apparent second lowest
bidder, and Green Bay Remodeling, Inc., apparent third lowest bidder; and
3. Rejecting the bid from Argos Construction as non-responsive; and
4. Accepting the bid of Paymun Building & Development, Inc. as the lowest
responsive and responsible bidder on the Project; and
5. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments,
extensions or other change orders until completion of the project in accordance
with the approved plans and specifications, with Paymun Building &
Development, Inc., for the K-Dock Restroom Renovation Project, in an amount
not to exceed $440,000, which includes a contract amount consisting of base bid
of $368,000, additive bid item in the amount of $32,000, and a 10% contingency
in the amount of $40,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding is available in the FY 2026 budget in the Marina Fund (Fund 608) and will be
recommended for appropriation through the FY 2026 first amendment to the
appropriations ordinance in the Measure T1 Bond Fund (Fund 511). No other funding is
required, and no other projects will be delayed due to this expenditure.

sections.current_situation:
Built in the 1960’s, K-Dock Restroom is an existing public restroom at the Berkeley
Waterfront that is in need of renovation in order to provide quality amenities to the
public. The following project elements will be renovated: two existing sinks will be
replaced with four sinks in total, two in the slip-holder’s restroom and two in the public
restroom; five toilets and a urinal will be replaced with six toilets, each in their own
fully walled-in room with mechanical ventilation; and two non-operational showers
will be replaced with a single new ADA accessible shower. Two infant changing stations
will be added and various restroom accessories will be added. A new bottle water filling
station will be installed on the outside of the K-Dock. Staff recommends that a contract
for this project be awarded to Paymun Building & Development, Inc.
sections.background:
The City conducted a competitive Invitation For Bid (IFB) process in July, 2025, and the
bid opening was on August 26, 2025. Thirteen (13) bids were received from a low bid of
$348,000 to a high bid $1,070,000 for the base bid work (see Attachment 2: Bid
Abstract). The City has determined that Paymun Building and Development, Inc. meets
the California public construction code definition of lowest responsive and responsible
bidder. References for Paymun Building and Development, Inc. proved satisfactory.
Staff therefore recommends the contract be awarded to Paymun Building and
Development, Inc.
On August 29, 2025, the City received a bid protest from ERA Construction Inc. (ERA),
the apparent fourth lowest bidder against Argos Construction, apparent lowest bidder,
Paymun Building & Development, Inc., apparent second lowest bidder, and Green Bay
Remodeling, Inc., apparent third lowest bidder. The City is required to comply with the
Project Manual, and specifically the Mandatory Bid Protest Procedures on Document 00
2114, Article 4, Section 4.01E, Page 00 2113-4 of the Project Manual, which states the
following: “Only Bidders who the City otherwise determines are responsive and
responsible are eligible to protest a Bid; protests from any other Bidder will not be
considered. In order to determine whether a protesting Bidder is responsive and
responsible, City may evaluate all information contained in any protesting Bidder’s Bid,
and conduct the same investigation and evaluation as City is entitled to take regarding
an Apparent Low Bidder.”
City staff evaluated ERA’s bid and deemed the bid non-responsive. Thus. ERA is
ineligible to protest the project. A copy of the bid evaluation letter is attached as
Attachment 3.

sections.environmental:
The construction contract includes requirements to comply with the City’s
Environmentally Preferable Purchasing Policy. The work will also comply with the
conditions contained in the Bay Conservation Development Commission (BCDC)
Permit, and City of Berkeley Building Permit.
As a maintenance project, this project meets the criteria for a Categorical Exemption
pursuant to CEQA Guidelines Section 15301 (Existing Facilities), since the project
involves minor alteration (renovation) of an existing public facility with no expansion of
existing use. On August 6, 2025, the City recorded a Notice of Exemption (NOE) for the
project with the Alameda County Clerk.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: PAYMUN BUILDING & DEVELOPMENT, INC. FOR THE K-DOCK
RESTROOM RENOVATION PROJECT
WHEREAS, the K-Dock Restroom, located at 201 University Avenue at the Berkeley
Waterfront is in need of renovation; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the labor nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
project; and
WHEREAS, an invitation for bids was duly advertised in July 23, 2025, bids were
opened on August 26, 2025, and the City received thirteen bids; and
WHEREAS, the City rejected the bid from ERA Construction, Inc., the fourth lowest bidder
and deemed ERA Construction, Inc ineligible to protest against Argos Construction,
apparent lowest bidder, Paymun Building & Development, Inc., apparent second lowest
bidder, and Green Bay Remodeling, Inc., apparent third lowest bidder; and
WHEREAS, the City rejected the bid from Argos Construction as non-responsive; and
WHEREAS, the City has determined that Paymun Building and Development, Inc. meets
the California public construction code definition of lowest responsible and responsible
bidder upon waiving inconsequential defects. References for Paymun Building and
Development, Inc. proved satisfactory; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the FY 2026 budget in the Marina Fund (Fund 608)
and will be recommended for appropriation through the FY 2026 first amendment to the
appropriations ordinance in the Measure T1 Bond Fund (Fund 511); and
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Plans and Specification No. 25-11743-C for the K-Dock Restroom Renovation Project are
approved.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley accepts the bid of
the lowest responsive and responsible bidder, Paymun Building and Development, Inc.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes the City
Manager to execute a contract and any amendment
